visa_types.name: Visit Visa
visa_types.documents_required:
Please refer to the Information leaflet below before submitting your application; this will  minimize chances of rejection of your Visa Application due to incorrect or  inadequate documents.
important notice: For other than Lebanese, kindly note that applicants can apply through our center with a valid annual residence permit in Lebanon only.
Instructions for  applicants below 18 years 
- For all children below       18years, both parents need to be present at the Visa Application Centre to       sign the VIDEX form. Children below 12years do not need to be present.
- In case one parent is out of the country during the application process, a signed official authorization form and passport copy with exit stamps from Lebanon must be provided to complete the process.
- All applicants below 18       years, travelling alone or with one parent must submit a signed official letter of       consent or authorization form and (from the notary) passport copy of the parent not       travelling.
In order to submit your application, please appear in person at your appointment. If you have already received a Schengen visa within the last 59 months and your fingerprints have been collected, you can authorize a third party to submit the application documents. Please fill the form and add your signature in the "Power of Attorney".
Information Leaflet:
Germany MB Visit LBN 
Germany MB Visit DEU-LBN 
Additional Information for Palestinian Nationals holding “Travel Document for Refugees”
Checklist for Syrian applicants without Residence Permit in Lebanon, please click here
visa_types.visa_fees:
[deu > lbn > en > Table:Airport Transit > Visa Fees 1]
Please note:
- The applicable fees are paid in  USD is as per the current exchange rate. It is subject to change without notice due to sudden foreign exchange fluctuations.
- Payments could be made via cash/card.
- All fees are non-refundable.
visa_types.processing_time:
The processing time for the application at the German Embassy is approximately 15 working days.
Please be advised that due to the above mentioned minimum processing time, we are not able to guarantee the return of passports by specific dates for travel.  
Please be sure to submit your  visa application well in advance, to avoid any inconveniences.
visa_types.overview:
Overview
Applicant will have to provide evidence that he/she falls under this category, kindly refer to the information sheet of documents required.
Please Note: 
- Effective 28 March 2017,       citizens of Georgia can travel to Schengen countries without a prior visa       for a period of up to 90 days. Please note that this exemption is only for       holders of a Georgian biometric passport.
- Effective 15 March 2016,       Peruvian nationals do not require a Schengen visa to travel to Germany for       a period of 90 days or less within a timeframe of 180 days.
- Effective 11 June 2017,       citizens of Ukraine can travel to Schengen countries without a prior visa       for a period of up to 90 days. Please note that this exemption is only for       holders of a Ukraine biometric passport. This does not apply  to       Ukrainian Nationals who are not holders of biometric passports       and travelling for work during the stay period of 90 days.
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
Applicant will have to provide evidence that he/she falls under this category, kindly refer to the information sheet of documents required.
Please Note: 
- Effective 28 March 2017,       citizens of Georgia can travel to Schengen countries without a prior visa       for a period of up to 90 days. Please note that this exemption is only for       holders of a Georgian biometric passport.
- Effective 15 March 2016,       Peruvian nationals do not require a Schengen visa to travel to Germany for       a period of 90 days or less within a timeframe of 180 days.
- Effective 11 June 2017,       citizens of Ukraine can travel to Schengen countries without a prior visa       for a period of up to 90 days. Please note that this exemption is only for       holders of a Ukraine biometric passport. This does not apply  to       Ukrainian Nationals who are not holders of biometric passports       and travelling for work during the stay period of 90 days.

## Visa Fees

[deu > lbn > en > Table:Airport Transit > Visa Fees 1]
Please note:
- The applicable fees are paid in  USD is as per the current exchange rate. It is subject to change without notice due to sudden foreign exchange fluctuations.
- Payments could be made via cash/card.
- All fees are non-refundable.

## Documents Required
Please refer to the Information leaflet below before submitting your application; this will  minimize chances of rejection of your Visa Application due to incorrect or  inadequate documents.
important notice: For other than Lebanese, kindly note that applicants can apply through our center with a valid annual residence permit in Lebanon only.
Instructions for  applicants below 18 years 
- For all children below       18years, both parents need to be present at the Visa Application Centre to       sign the VIDEX form. Children below 12years do not need to be present.
- In case one parent is out of the country during the application process, a signed official authorization form and passport copy with exit stamps from Lebanon must be provided to complete the process.
- All applicants below 18       years, travelling alone or with one parent must submit a signed official letter of       consent or authorization form and (from the notary) passport copy of the parent not       travelling.
In order to submit your application, please appear in person at your appointment. If you have already received a Schengen visa within the last 59 months and your fingerprints have been collected, you can authorize a third party to submit the application documents. Please fill the form and add your signature in the "Power of Attorney".
Information Leaflet:
Germany MB Visit LBN 
Germany MB Visit DEU-LBN 
Additional Information for Palestinian Nationals holding “Travel Document for Refugees”
Checklist for Syrian applicants without Residence Permit in Lebanon, please click here

## Photo Specifications
Two recent biometric passport sized photographs (3.5x4,5 cm) as per specifications.
Sample of Biometric Photo
Requirements: 
- NEW! Should NOT be older than 6 months
- A frontal view of the face covering 70-80% (32-26mm)       of the photo
- A uniform and white-colored background
- Clear facial features and eyes – not covered by hair       or glasses frame
- Head coverings are only accepted for religious       reasons
- Do not glue or staple the pictures to the       application form

## Processing Time
The processing time for the application at the German Embassy is approximately 15 working days.
Please be advised that due to the above mentioned minimum processing time, we are not able to guarantee the return of passports by specific dates for travel.  
Please be sure to submit your  visa application well in advance, to avoid any inconveniences.

## Online Application Form
Below are the  forms that are required to be filled to submit your visa application:
Schengen Visa Application Form (https://videx.diplo.de/videx/visum-erfassung/#/videx-kurzfristiger-aufenthalt) (To be  filled online - mandatory)
The Schengen Visa Application form must be  filled either in English/German. 
Instructions to complete an online form: 
- Click  on the form link above
- When  the page opens complete the form
- Once  complete print out the form on clear paper
- The complete printed set of your application form including the last barcoded page of your application form should be presented while submitting your application.
* The  barcode page contains the necessary data in condensed and machine readable  format.
*** If you are not able to fill the form online, a form filling service is available at the Visa Application Centre.

visa_types.name: SCHENGEN VISA, CATEGORY C
visa_types.overview:
- Airport Transit
- Business/conference/trade Fair
- Medical Treatment
- Tourist Visa
- Visit Visa
- Schengen Rules:
- The visa application has to be presented to the Embassy/Consulate of the country of main destination  (determined by the purpose of the journey and the length of stay).
- If your main destination cannot be ascertained, the Schengen state through which you are staying the longest is responsible for granting the visa, in case you are staying equally in more than one Schengen country, the country with the first entry is responsible for granting the visa.
- Do  I need a Visa for Germany?
- Click here (https://www.auswaertiges-amt.de/de/service/visa-und-aufenthalt/staatenliste-zur-visumpflicht/207820) to check if you  require a visa to enter Germany.
- Schengen Visa and Visa Code:
- In order to apply for a Schengen visa, the applicant needs to provide the  documents as per the purpose of the trip. In individual cases, additional  documents may be requested. Presentation of the documents does not  automatically lead to the issuance of a visa and possession of a visa does not  confer an automatic right of entry. There is no automatic entitlement or legal  right to a Schengen visa.The ultimate decision regarding admission to the  Schengen territory lies with the border authorities upon arrival.
- The Visa Code standardizes the visa requirements which must be examined by  the mission in the course of the visa procedure. The respective mission makes a  decision on the visa application at its own discretion, taking into account all  the circumstances in any given individual case.
- All visa applicants for Germany are advised to schedule an appointment and  visit the Joint Visa Application Centre in Lebanon  to submit their applications, together with  all necessary documents
- Processing time for a visa may take up to 15 working days. Applicants are therefore  requested to organise themselves accordingly taking into consideration possible  waiting periods due to the online appointment system.
- The purpose of the proposed visit may not be altered once the visa has been  issued. This is also applicable after entering the Schengen territory.
- Above is regular information, if you wish to continue and to know more  about the Visa application process, kindly click here (https://www.auswaertiges-amt.de/de).
- Information on this website is subject to change, kindly check the  information for updates before you apply.

- If your main destination cannot be ascertained, the Schengen state through which you are staying the longest is responsible for granting the visa, in case you are staying equally in more than one Schengen country, the country with the first entry is responsible for granting the visa.
- Do  I need a Visa for Germany?
- Click here (https://www.auswaertiges-amt.de/de/service/visa-und-aufenthalt/staatenliste-zur-visumpflicht/207820) to check if you  require a visa to enter Germany.
- Schengen Visa and Visa Code:
- In order to apply for a Schengen visa, the applicant needs to provide the  documents as per the purpose of the trip. In individual cases, additional  documents may be requested. Presentation of the documents does not  automatically lead to the issuance of a visa and possession of a visa does not  confer an automatic right of entry. There is no automatic entitlement or legal  right to a Schengen visa.The ultimate decision regarding admission to the  Schengen territory lies with the border authorities upon arrival.
- The Visa Code standardizes the visa requirements which must be examined by  the mission in the course of the visa procedure. The respective mission makes a  decision on the visa application at its own discretion, taking into account all  the circumstances in any given individual case.
- All visa applicants for Germany are advised to schedule an appointment and  visit the Joint Visa Application Centre in Lebanon  to submit their applications, together with  all necessary documents
- Processing time for a visa may take up to 15 working days. Applicants are therefore  requested to organise themselves accordingly taking into consideration possible  waiting periods due to the online appointment system.
- The purpose of the proposed visit may not be altered once the visa has been  issued. This is also applicable after entering the Schengen territory.
- Above is regular information, if you wish to continue and to know more  about the Visa application process, kindly click here (https://www.auswaertiges-amt.de/de).

visa_types.name: National Visa D
visa_types.overview:
National visas for stays of more than 90 days in Germany (i.e., Long-Term Visas, Category D) can generally only be submitted at the German Embassy directly and with an appointment.
For more general information on national visas please click here (https://beirut.diplo.de/lb-ar/service/visa-einreise/-/2088854) to access the Embassy’s website (Arabic version).
Decisions are made by the Embassy, who needs a confirmation of the competent Foreigners’ Office in Germany before issuing your visa. You will be informed automatically when the decision has been made and your passport is ready to be shipped to you by VFS. The process usually takes approximately 4 weeks upon handing in a complete file at our center.
This process is only available for persons (actually) residing in Lebanon. Your visa can only be issued to you in Lebanon.
Please read the Embassies information sheets on student visas carefully before starting to prepare your documents:
Information and Application Forms:
All applicants applying for national visa (category D) will have to fill the VIDEX on https://videx-national.diplo.de (https://videx-national.diplo.de/).
For general information on long-term student visas in Arabic, please click here
For information leaflets and application forms for print out (optional) please click here (https://beirut.diplo.de/blob/2120680/68cef2d19b031451b742819d016cf2ce/download-datei-antrag-nationale-visa-deutscharabisch-data.pdf)

visa_types.name: Mandatory internship Visa
visa_types.documents_required:
Supporting documents as mentioned in the following links are required.
Internship Checklist
visa_types.visa_fees:
[deu > lbn > en > Table:Mandatory internship Visa > Visa Fees 1]
Fees are paid in USD.
Visa fees are non refundable.
visa_types.overview:
Overview
National or Long-Term Visas (Category D) for Germany are issued at the Consulate General of Germany, Beirut, applications can be submitted only through prior appointments.
The Consulate General is only allowed to issue your visa, if a confirmation has been issued by the Foreign Nationals Authority in Germany.
Please ensure all documentation is prepared as per the respective information leaflets before your visit.
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
National or Long-Term Visas (Category D) for Germany are issued at the Consulate General of Germany, Beirut, applications can be submitted only through prior appointments.
The Consulate General is only allowed to issue your visa, if a confirmation has been issued by the Foreign Nationals Authority in Germany.
Please ensure all documentation is prepared as per the respective information leaflets before your visit.

## Visa Fees

[deu > lbn > en > Table:Mandatory internship Visa > Visa Fees 1]
Fees are paid in USD.
Visa fees are non refundable.

## Documents Required
Supporting documents as mentioned in the following links are required.
Internship Checklist

## Application Form
The visa application form must be completed using Videx. Please click here to start filling your National Visa Application Form (https://videx.diplo.de/videx/visum-erfassung/#/videx-kurzfristiger-aufenthalt)
The visa application form can also be completed in German or English for each application and submitted along with the supporting documents on the day of appointment.

## Photo Specifications
Two recent biometric passport sized photographs (3.5x4,5 cm) as per specifications.
Sample of Biometric Photo
Requirements: 
- NEW! Should NOT be older than 6 months
- A frontal view of the face covering 70-80% (32-26mm)       of the photo
- A uniform and white-colored background
- Clear facial features and eyes – not covered by hair       or glasses frame
- Head coverings are only accepted for religious       reasons
- Do not glue or staple the pictures to the       application form

visa_types.overview:
Overview
Applicant will have  to provide evidence that he/she falls under this category, kindly refer to the  information sheet of documents required. 
Under the Business  visa category the following cases would be applicable:
- Business matters/meetings/       negotiation/trainings
- Conference/Congress/Exhibition
- Scientific/Research events
- Journalists
- Official delegations
Please Note:
- Effective 28 March 2017,       citizens of Georgia can travel to Schengen countries without a prior visa       for a period of up to 90 days. Please note that this exemption is only for       holders of a Georgian biometric passport.

This does not apply  to       Ukrainian Nationals who are not holders of biometric passports       and travelling for work during the stay period of 90 days.

Schengen Visa Application Form (https://videx.diplo.de/videx/visum-erfassung/#/videx-kurzfristiger-aufenthalt) (To be  filled online - mandatory)
The Schengen Visa Application form must be  filled either in English/German. 
Instructions to complete an online form: 
- Click  on the form link above
- When  the page opens complete the form
- Once  complete print out the form on clear paper
- The complete printed set of your application form including the last barcoded page of your application form should be presented while submitting your application.
* The  barcode page contains the necessary data in condensed and machine readable  format.
** If you are not able to fill the form online, a form filling service is available at the Visa Application Centre.

visa_types.name: Tourist Visa
visa_types.documents_required:
Please refer to the Information leaflet below before submitting your application; this will  minimize chances of rejection of your Visa Application due to incorrect or  inadequate documents.
important notice: For other than Lebanese, kindly note that applicants can apply through our center with a valid annual residence permit in Lebanon only.
Instructions for  applicants below 18 years 
- For all children below       18years, both parents need to be present at the Visa Application Centre to       sign the VIDEX form. Children below 12years do not need to be present.
- In case one parent is out of the country during the application process, a signed official authorization form and passport copy with exit stamps from Lebanonmust be provided to complete the process.
- All applicants below 18       years, travelling alone or with one parent must submit a signed official letter of       consent or authorization form (from the notary) and passport copy of the parent not       travelling.
In order to submit your application, please appear in person at your appointment. If you have already received a Schengen visa within the last 59 months and your fingerprints have been collected, you can authorize a third party to submit the application documents. Please fill the form and add your signature in the "Power of Attorney".
Information Leaflet:
Germany MB Tourism LBN
Germany MB Tourism DEU-LBN

visa_types.overview:
Overview
Applicant will have to provide evidence that he/she falls under this category, kindly refer to the information sheet of documents required
Please Note: 
- Effective 28 March 2017, citizens of Georgia can travel to Schengen countries without a prior visa for a period of up to 90 days. Please note that this exemption is only for holders of a Georgian biometric passport.
- Effective 15 March 2016, Peruvian nationals do not require a Schengen visa to travel to Germany for a period of 90 days or less within a timeframe of 180 days.
- Effective 11 June 2017, citizens of Ukraine can travel to Schengen countries without a prior visa for a period of up to 90 days. Please note that this exemption is only for holders of a Ukraine biometric passport. This does not apply  to Ukrainian Nationals who are not holders of biometric passports and travelling for work during the stay period of 90 days.
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
Applicant will have to provide evidence that he/she falls under this category, kindly refer to the information sheet of documents required
Please Note: 
- Effective 28 March 2017, citizens of Georgia can travel to Schengen countries without a prior visa for a period of up to 90 days. Please note that this exemption is only for holders of a Georgian biometric passport.
- Effective 15 March 2016, Peruvian nationals do not require a Schengen visa to travel to Germany for a period of 90 days or less within a timeframe of 180 days.
- Effective 11 June 2017, citizens of Ukraine can travel to Schengen countries without a prior visa for a period of up to 90 days. Please note that this exemption is only for holders of a Ukraine biometric passport. This does not apply  to Ukrainian Nationals who are not holders of biometric passports and travelling for work during the stay period of 90 days.

## Visa Fees

[deu > lbn > en > Table:Airport Transit > Visa Fees 1]
Please note:
- The applicable fees are paid in  USD is as per the current exchange rate. It is subject to change without notice due to sudden foreign exchange fluctuations.
- Payments could be made via cash/card.
- All fees are non-refundable.

## Documents Required
Please refer to the Information leaflet below before submitting your application; this will  minimize chances of rejection of your Visa Application due to incorrect or  inadequate documents.
important notice: For other than Lebanese, kindly note that applicants can apply through our center with a valid annual residence permit in Lebanon only.
Instructions for  applicants below 18 years 
- For all children below       18years, both parents need to be present at the Visa Application Centre to       sign the VIDEX form. Children below 12years do not need to be present.
- In case one parent is out of the country during the application process, a signed official authorization form and passport copy with exit stamps from Lebanonmust be provided to complete the process.
- All applicants below 18       years, travelling alone or with one parent must submit a signed official letter of       consent or authorization form (from the notary) and passport copy of the parent not       travelling.
In order to submit your application, please appear in person at your appointment. If you have already received a Schengen visa within the last 59 months and your fingerprints have been collected, you can authorize a third party to submit the application documents. Please fill the form and add your signature in the "Power of Attorney".
Information Leaflet:
Germany MB Tourism LBN
Germany MB Tourism DEU-LBN 
Additional Information for Palestinian Nationals holding “Travel Document for Refugees”
Checklist for Syrian applicants without Residence Permit in Lebanon, please click here

## Photo Specifications
Two recent biometric passport sized photographs (3.5x4,5 cm) as per specifications.
Sample of Biometric Photo
Requirements: 
- NEW! Should NOT be older than 6 months
- A frontal view of the face covering 70-80% (32-26mm)       of the photo
- A uniform and white-colored background
- Clear facial features and eyes – not covered by hair       or glasses frame
- Head coverings are only accepted for religious       reasons
- Do not glue or staple the pictures to the       application form

## Processing Time
The processing time for the application at the German Embassy is approximately 15 working days.
Please be advised that due to the above mentioned minimum processing time, we are not able to guarantee the return of passports by specific dates for travel. 
Please be sure to submit your  visa application well in advance, to avoid any inconveniences.

## Online Application Form
Below are the  forms that are required to be filled to submit your visa application:
Schengen Visa Application Form (https://videx.diplo.de/videx/visum-erfassung/#/videx-kurzfristiger-aufenthalt) (To be  filled online - mandatory)
The Schengen Visa Application form must be  filled either in English/German. 
Instructions to complete an online form: 
- Click  on the form link above
- When  the page opens complete the form
- Once  complete print out the form on clear paper
- The  complete the printed set of your application form including the last barcoded  page of your application form should be presented while submitting your  application.
* The  barcode page contains the necessary data in condensed and machine readable  format.
** If you are not able to fill the form online, a form filling service is available at the Visa Application.

visa_types.overview:
Overview
If you want to study in Germany and already hold an unconditional acceptance letter of your German University (No conditions are required), please book your appointment our call center.
Applications are accepted at the Visa Application Center from Monday to Friday through mandatory appointments. In order to arrange for your appointment, please call on the following link (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/lbn/en/deu/login).
Students who still need to legalize their documents will be assisted through giving them the option to legalize their documents on Mondays within the working hours at the visa application center along with a Visa Appointment two weeks post the legalization date provided through our agents on helpline number 00961 1 743 643.
Please ensure all documentation is prepared as per the respective information leaflets (one set of originals and two set of copies) before your visit.
 Information on National Long term D Visa
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
If you want to study in Germany and already hold an unconditional acceptance letter of your German University (No conditions are required), please book your appointment our call center.
Applications are accepted at the Visa Application Center from Monday to Friday through mandatory appointments. In order to arrange for your appointment, please call on the following link (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/lbn/en/deu/login).
Students who still need to legalize their documents will be assisted through giving them the option to legalize their documents on Mondays within the working hours at the visa application center along with a Visa Appointment two weeks post the legalization date provided through our agents on helpline number 00961 1 743 643.
Please ensure all documentation is prepared as per the respective information leaflets (one set of originals and two set of copies) before your visit.
 Information on National Long term D Visa

## Visa Fees

[deu > lbn > en > Table:Airport Transit > Visa Fees 1]

Study

## Application Form
The visa application form must be completed using Videx. Please click here to start filling your National Visa Application Form (https://videx-national.diplo.de/videx/visum-erfassung/#/videx-langfristiger-aufenthalt)
The visa application form can also be completed in German or English for each application and submitted along with the supporting documents on the day of appointment
Click here (https://beirut.diplo.de/blob/2120680/68cef2d19b031451b742819d016cf2ce/download-datei-antrag-nationale-visa-deutscharabisch-data.pdf) to download and print the Visa application form (in PDF)

## More Information

Two recent biometric passport sized photographs (3.5x4,5 cm) as per specifications.
Sample of Biometric Photo
Requirements: 
- NEW! Should NOT be older than 6 months
- A frontal view of the face covering 70-80% (32-26mm)       of the photo
- A uniform and white-colored background
- Clear facial features and eyes – not covered by hair       or glasses frame
- Head coverings are only accepted for religious       reasons
- Do not glue or staple the pictures to the       application form

visa_types.overview:
Overview
For Persons  applying for Airport Transit Visa
Entry clearance  requirements depend on the citizenship and resident status of the applicant  (sometimes in a third country). Only a flight through one Schengen Union Country at a time  is considered as a transit. The vast majority of travelers do not need a visa  when air transiting through Germany. In Germany, the following airports have an  International Transit Area: Cologne/Bonn (transit only possible between 4:40 am and 11:00 pm),  Düsseldorf (transit only possible between  6:00 am and 9:00 pm and only if the airline has arranged the transit with  airport security – please check with your airline), Frankfurt/Main, Hamburg (limited time frame only 4:40 am  to 11:40 pm), Munich (München).
Please check with  your travel agent / airline, if your flight is going via another airport. If  you leave the airport’s international transit area, i.e. change the terminal,  you will have to apply for a Schengen Visa – Category C.
Please Note: 
- Effective 28 March 2017,       citizens of Georgia can travel to Schengen countries without a prior visa       for a period of up to 90 days. Please note that this exemption is only for       holders of a Georgian biometric passport.
- Effective 15 March 2016,       Peruvian nationals do not require a Schengen visa to travel to Germany for       a period of 90 days or less within a timeframe of 180 days.
- Effective 11 June 2017,       citizens of Ukraine can travel to Schengen countries without a prior visa       for a period of up to 90 days. Please note that this exemption is only for       holders of a Ukraine biometric passport. This does not apply  to       Ukrainian Nationals who are not holders of biometric passports       and travelling for work during the stay period of 90 days.
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
For Persons  applying for Airport Transit Visa
Entry clearance  requirements depend on the citizenship and resident status of the applicant  (sometimes in a third country). Only a flight through one Schengen Union Country at a time  is considered as a transit. The vast majority of travelers do not need a visa  when air transiting through Germany. In Germany, the following airports have an  International Transit Area: Cologne/Bonn (transit only possible between 4:40 am and 11:00 pm),  Düsseldorf (transit only possible between  6:00 am and 9:00 pm and only if the airline has arranged the transit with  airport security – please check with your airline), Frankfurt/Main, Hamburg (limited time frame only 4:40 am  to 11:40 pm), Munich (München).
Please check with  your travel agent / airline, if your flight is going via another airport. If  you leave the airport’s international transit area, i.e. change the terminal,  you will have to apply for a Schengen Visa – Category C.
Please Note: 
- Effective 28 March 2017,       citizens of Georgia can travel to Schengen countries without a prior visa       for a period of up to 90 days. Please note that this exemption is only for       holders of a Georgian biometric passport.
- Effective 15 March 2016,       Peruvian nationals do not require a Schengen visa to travel to Germany for       a period of 90 days or less within a timeframe of 180 days.
- Effective 11 June 2017,       citizens of Ukraine can travel to Schengen countries without a prior visa       for a period of up to 90 days. Please note that this exemption is only for       holders of a Ukraine biometric passport. This does not apply  to       Ukrainian Nationals who are not holders of biometric passports       and travelling for work during the stay period of 90 days.

## Visa Fees

important notice: For other than Lebanese, kindly note that applicants can apply through our center with a valid annual residence permit in Lebanon only.

- All applicants below 18       years, travelling alone or with one parent must submit a signed official letter of       consent or authorization form and (from the notary) passport copy of the parent not       travelling.
In order to submit your application, please appear in person at your appointment. If you have already received a Schengen visa within the last 59 months and your fingerprints have been collected, you can authorize a third party to submit the application documents. Please fill the form and add your signature in the "Power of Attorney".

## Processing Time

Please be advised that due to the above mentioned minimum processing time, we are not able to guarantee the return of passports by specific dates for travel.  
Please be sure to submit your visa application well in advance, to avoid  any inconveniences.

visa_types.overview:
Overview
National or Long-Term Visas (Category D) for Germany are issued at the Consulate General of Germany, Beirut, applications can be submitted only through prior appointments.
The Consulate General is only allowed to issue your visa, if a confirmation has been issued by the Foreign Nationals Authority in Germany. 
Please ensure all documentation is prepared as per the respective information leaflets before your visit.
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
National or Long-Term Visas (Category D) for Germany are issued at the Consulate General of Germany, Beirut, applications can be submitted only through prior appointments.
The Consulate General is only allowed to issue your visa, if a confirmation has been issued by the Foreign Nationals Authority in Germany. 
Please ensure all documentation is prepared as per the respective information leaflets before your visit.

## Visa Fees

[deu > lbn > en > Table:Mandatory internship Visa > Visa Fees 1]
Fees are paid in USD.
Visa fees are non refundable.

## Documents Required
Work Visa with pre-approval from the Federal Employment Agency

## Application Form
The visa application form must be completed using Videx. Please click here to start filling your National Visa Application Form (https://videx-national.diplo.de/videx/visum-erfassung/#/videx-langfristiger-aufenthalt)
The visa application form can also be completed in German or English for each application and submitted along with the supporting documents on the day of appointment.

[deu > lbn > en > Table:Work Visa with pre-approval from the Federal Employment Agency Visa > Application Form 1]

## More Information
Kindly click here (https://beirut.diplo.de/lb-ar/service/visa-einreise/-/2088438) for more information on National Visas

## Photo Specifications
Two recent biometric passport sized photographs (3.5x4,5 cm) as per specifications.
Sample of Biometric Photo
Requirements: 
- NEW! Should NOT be older than 6 months
- A frontal view of the face covering 70-80% (32-26mm)       of the photo
- A uniform and white-colored background
- Clear facial features and eyes – not covered by hair       or glasses frame
- Head coverings are only accepted for religious       reasons
